mnemonics.title: Try-Except Execution Order
mnemonics.content: Try First, Except If Failed, Else Optional, Finally Always - TEEF: Try → Except → Else → Finally

mnemonics.title: Exception Hierarchy Remembering
mnemonics.content: BaseException is the ROOT, Exception is the BRANCH, RuntimeError/ValueError are the LEAVES - think family tree from top (Base) to bottom (specific)

mnemonics.title: Catch Specific First
mnemonics.content: Specific SPiCE catches first: Syntax errors, Pragma, i - Wait, think 'More Specific Catches First' - Catch child exceptions before parent exceptions

mnemonics.title: Raise Purpose
mnemonics.content: Raise Your Hand When Something Goes Wrong - raise keyword signals an error has occurred

mnemonics.title: Finally Always Runs
mnemonics.content: Finally = Faithful Friend - no matter what happens in try/except, finally executes always like a loyal friend

acronyms.expansion: Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission
acronyms.usage: Python's philosophy - try code first, handle exceptions if they occur (try-except) rather than checking beforehand (LBYL)
Checking conditions before executing code (if statements) - opposite of EAFP, less Pythonic
In try-except: TRY block contains test code, if it yields an exception, except block handles it
Keyword to manually trigger an exception when error condition is detected in code

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a safety net under a trapeze artist (try block). If they fall (exception), the net catches them (except block) so they don't hit the ground (program crash)
Think of a tree - BaseException at the trunk, Exception as main branch, specific errors (ValueError, TypeError) as smaller branches and leaves
Picture a cleanup crew (finally) that ALWAYS arrives after a party (try-except), regardless of what happened - they clean up whether the party was successful or failed
Think of raising your hand in class to signal something is wrong - you're signaling to the program that an error condition exists
Imagine a specialized doctor (custom exception class) for a specific disease - more precise diagnosis than a general practitioner

analogies.analogy: Like a doctor's try-catch - they try a treatment (try block), if it doesn't work (exception), they catch the problem and try a different treatment (except block)
Like animal classification - BaseException is 'Animal', Exception is 'Mammal', ValueError is 'Carnivore' - more specific as you go down
Like the cleanup crew at a restaurant - whether the dinner goes well or customers complain (exceptions), the crew always cleans up after closing
Like sounding a fire alarm - you're raising/announcing that something is wrong and people need to respond (handle the exception)
Like different emergency response teams - fire department for fires (ValueError), ambulance for medical emergencies (TypeError), police for crimes (FileNotFoundError)
Like specialized error codes in a hospital - instead of generic 'error', you have specific errors like 'SugarTooHighError' or 'HeartRateLowError' for precise handling
